_comment: Phase 12 mutation testing — see AGENTS.md §5.3 + docs/MUTATION_REPORT.md. Uses vitest.node.config.ts (Node env, the same config whose 100% line coverage gate we're auditing). `src/index.ts`, `src/env.ts`, and `src/routes/**` (Hono glue) are excluded per §5.2.
_ratchet_comment: Break threshold is the current measured mutation score (floored). Any PR that lowers it fails. To RAISE the baseline, close surviving mutants (see docs/MUTATION_REPORT.md top-gaps list), re-run `vp run mutation`, and update `break` here in the same PR. `low` is a +3 ratchet target (warns, does not fail). `high` is a +8 stretch goal. NOTE (2026-07-17, passkey Phase A PR #841 rebase onto main): PR #841 initially lowered break 90→84 (auth-do.ts + room.ts gate additions, ~91%→84.1%). After rebasing onto main (which restored break to 90) and four rounds of targeted test-only hardening — auth-do.ts WebAuthn ceremony/session/challenge coverage (87→15 survivors, 77.55%→96.17%), auth-session.ts/authorize.ts/ws-upgrade.ts policy coverage (authorize.ts 14→2 survivors, 98.20%), and rate-limit.ts/room-create-limit.ts boundary coverage (26→19 and 30→19 survivors respectively) — the authoritative full-worker Stryker run measures 90.20% combined, clearing this 90 floor. Fresh main-baseline comparison run this session (clean origin/main worktree, same mutate scope minus auth-do.ts/session files which don't exist on main): 90.16%. `break` was kept at 90 throughout the hardening effort — never silently lowered while red. Top remaining survivor clusters (full-worker run, ~4415 mutants, mutate scope src/handlers/**, src/lib/**, src/room.ts, src/auth-do.ts, !src/lib/webauthn-ops.ts): room.ts 183 survivors (86.41% file score, largest single cluster, deliberately left untouched this round — a dedicated hardening pass confirmed the other clusters' kills were sufficient to clear the floor without it; cluster map — armAlarm/housekeeping-alarm boolean/conditional mutants, storage.list ObjectLiteral prefix-arg mutants, chunk-cleanup loop bounds, init-private ACL/JSON guards — preserved for a future ratchet-raise pass), xlsx-import.ts 46, xlsx-build.ts 21, rate-limit.ts 19, room-create-limit.ts 19, auth-do.ts 15 (concentrated in ~8 documented clusters, several confirmed mathematically/behaviorally equivalent per auth-do.node.test.ts's own commit messages), snapshot-storage.ts 10, auth-session.ts 10, ws-upgrade.ts 9, sandstorm-access.ts 8. Note: ~27 of the ~4415 total mutants are Timeout-classified, introducing roughly ±0.02pt of run-to-run measurement noise at the margin — the 90.20 vs 90.16 comparison is a thin but real pass, not a wide-margin one; see docs/MUTATION_REPORT.md for the full addendum.
inPlace_comment: Skip the tsconfig-preprocessor (and its `import('typescript')`) — we only run Stryker in CI and git worktrees, both of which are disposable, so a mid-run crash cannot damage real sources.
